problem_statement.title: DRA scheduler plugin cannot fulfill gang scheduling with shared ResourceClaims
problem_statement.description:
### What happened?

When two Pods are created in a gang where both Pods share the same ResourceClaim, the scheduler falls into an endless loop trying to schedule the Pods and never succeeds.

```
Events:
  Type     Reason            Age                          From               Message
  ----     ------            ----                         ----               -------
  Warning  FailedScheduling  2m21s (x2292168 over 7m21s)  default-scheduler  0/2 nodes are available: resource claim default/single-gpu is in the process of being allocated. no new claims to deallocate, preemption: 0/2 nodes are available: 2 Preemption is not helpful for scheduling.
```

### What did you expect to happen?

Both Pods schedule with the shared ResourceClaim.

### How can we reproduce it (as minimally and precisely as possible)?

Create a kind cluster:
```
kind build node-image --image kindest/node:latest
kind create cluster --image kindest/node:latest --config kind.yaml
```
<details>
<summary>kind.yaml</summary>

```yaml
kind: Cluster
apiVersion: kind.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
nodes:
- role: control-plane
- role: worker
kubeadmConfigPatches:
  - |
    kind: ClusterConfiguration
    apiVersion: kubeadm.k8s.io/v1beta3
    apiServer:
        extraArgs:
          runtime-config: "scheduling.k8s.io/v1alpha2=true"
featureGates:
  GenericWorkload: true
  GangScheduling: true
```

</details>

Install a DRA driver:
```
helm upgrade --install dra-example-driver oci://registry.k8s.io/dra-example-driver/charts/dra-example-driver -n dra-example-driver --create-namespace
```

Create a ResourceClaim, PodGroup, and Pods in the PodGroup sharing the claim:
<details>
<summary>workload.yaml</summary>

```yaml
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: single-gpu
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: gpu
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: gpu.example.com
---
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1alpha2
kind: PodGroup
metadata:
  name: podgroup
spec:
  schedulingPolicy:
    gang:
      minCount: 2
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
    spec:
      resourceClaims:
        - name: claim
          resourceClaimName: single-gpu
      containers:
        - name: ctr
          image: ubuntu:22.04
          command: ["bash", "-c"]
          args: ["export; trap 'exit 0' TERM; sleep 9999 & wait"]
          resources:
            claims:
              - name: claim
      schedulingGroup:
        podGroupName: podgroup
```
</details>

### Anything else we need to know?

Both Pods will schedule together if either of the following changes are made:
- Set the PodGroup's `spec.schedulingPolicy` to `basic: {}`
- Create separate ResourceClaims for each Pod instead of sharing (ResourceClaimTemplate).

### Kubernetes version

<details>

```console
$ kubectl version
Client Version: v1.35.1
Kustomize Version: v5.7.1
Server Version: v1.36.0-alpha.2.435+0009958799e560
```

</details>


### Cloud provider

<details>
Local kind cluster
</details>


### OS version

Kind node:
<details>

```console
$ cat /etc/os-release
PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
NAME="Debian GNU/Linux"
VERSION_ID="12"
VERSION="12 (bookworm)"
VERSION_CODENAME=bookworm
ID=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.debian.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://www.debian.org/support"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.debian.org/"
$ uname -a
Linux kind-control-plane 6.17.0-1008-azure #8~24.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Mon Jan 26 18:35:40 UTC 2026 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

</details>


### Install tools

<details>

```
% kind version
kind v0.31.0 go1.26.1 linux/amd64
```
</details>


### Container runtime (CRI) and version (if applicable)

<details>

</details>


### Related plugins (CNI, CSI, ...) and versions (if applicable)

<details>
DRA Example Driver v0.2.1
</details>
